params.text:
Welcome to PEG Feeding

Welcome to PEG Feeding. Some of the people you support cannot eat and drink safely by mouth, and instead receive their food, fluids and medicines straight into the stomach through a feeding tube. This awareness course helps you understand what a PEG is, why people are fed this way, and how careful, respectful support keeps them safe, comfortable and nourished.

  • Understand: what a PEG is and why people are fed enterally.
  • Support safely: the everyday care that surrounds a feeding tube.
  • Escalate: when and how to report concerns to a nurse or dietitian.

Awareness only — know your limits

PEG feeding is a delegated clinical task. This course raises your awareness only. You may only carry out PEG feeding, flushing or giving medication through a PEG if you have had task-specific training, been assessed as competent, and the task has been delegated to you by a registered nurse or dietitian and set out in the person's care plan.

This course does not authorise you to carry out PEG feeding without task-specific training, assessed competence and delegation by a registered nurse or dietitian. If you are ever unsure about a tube, or it has been dislodged, you must never restart the feed — you stop and escalate. Throughout, remember that your two most important skills are noticing changes and reporting them promptly.
